Title: Analysis genome of Pseudotaxus chienii reveals insights into the origin and evolution of taxane biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial committed step of taxane core formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies the taxane core precursor for downstream taxane/taxusin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate to taxa-4(5),11(12)-diene, initiating taxane biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I diterpene synthase (TPS); DDXXD/E and (N,D)DXX(S,T)XXXE metal-binding motifs; lacks canonical DXDD class II motif
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: taxane biosynthesis-related genes were upregulated by MeJA; specific TS1 tissue pattern not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables taxadiene production and supports the existence of an initial taxane biosynthetic capacity in P. chienii.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; heterologous transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS detection of taxadiene; protein structural modeling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment induces taxane biosynthesis-related genes in P. chienii
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Taxusin is an industrial precursor/substrate for production of taxoids including Taxol derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcTS1 is a functional taxadiene synthase from P. chienii. 2. Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana produced taxadiene detected by GC-MS. 3. PcTS1 retains conserved class I metal-binding catalytic motifs, indicating conservation of TS catalytic architecture across Pseudotaxus and Taxus.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for heterologous taxadiene production and enzyme engineering to improve TS activity through conserved metal-ion catalytic sites.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly: GCA_053640655.1; genomic raw data: PRJNA1205499; transcriptome: GSE288094; metabolomics/MS imaging project: m-2025
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper also mentions PcTS2 in phylogeny, but only PcTS1 is functionally validated as a core gene.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: modifies taxusin/taxane intermediates toward hydroxylated taxanes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxane/taxusin intermediates at the 2α position.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, CYP725-related taxane hydroxylase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: taxane biosynthesis-related genes are significantly upregulated by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports conversion of taxusin into more hydroxylated taxanes in P. chienii, consistent with the taxusin branch of the partial pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by substrate feeding with taxusin and LC-MS/MS; comparative activity assay versus T. mairei homolog
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential precursor pool for industrial taxoid production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P. chienii contains a functional T2OH gene. 2. Feeding assays showed PcT2OH activity similar to the T. mairei homolog. 3. Presence of T2OH supports diversion of flux toward hydroxylated taxusin derivatives in the absence of downstream TBT/TOT1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be incorporated into synthetic biology routes for hydroxylated taxane production and pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The exact product spectrum beyond 2α hydroxylation is inferred from pathway context.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: modifies taxusin/taxane intermediates toward hydroxylated taxanes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxane/taxusin intermediates at the 7β position.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, CYP725-related taxane hydroxylase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: taxane biosynthesis-related genes are significantly upregulated by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports formation of hydroxylated taxusin derivatives in P. chienii and helps explain accumulation of alternative taxane end products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by substrate feeding with taxusin and LC-MS/MS; comparative activity assay versus T. mairei homolog
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential precursor pool for industrial taxoid production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P. chienii contains a functional T7OH gene. 2. Feeding assays showed PcT7OH activity similar to the T. mairei homolog. 3. Together with T2OH, T7OH supports the alternative taxusin branch in the incomplete P. chienii taxane pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway reconstruction of hydroxylated taxane derivatives in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper interprets the presence of both T2OH and T7OH as enabling hydroxylation of taxusin to 2α,7β-dihydroxytaxusin.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation of 10-deacetylbaccatin III and extension to complete Taxol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 7,13-diacetylbaccatin III leading toward 10-deacetylbaccatin III/baccatin III branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a benzoyl group to the 2α-hydroxyl of a late taxane intermediate, enabling downstream formation of 10-deacetylbaccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; essential missing step in P. chienii
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its absence blocks the P. chienii pathway before 10-deacetylbaccatin III formation and redirects metabolic flux toward taxusin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics/pathway reconstruction; metabolomics showing absence of 10-deacetylbaccatin III in P. chienii and high taxusin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Paclitaxel is an anticancer chemotherapeutic; taxusin is an industrial precursor taxoid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TBT was not detected in the P. chienii genome. 2. The pathway in P. chienii terminates before TBT. 3. Loss/absence of TBT is proposed as a principal cause of taxusin accumulation and inability to synthesize 10-deacetylbaccatin III and downstream Taxol intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime engineering target to complete the P. chienii pathway; introduction/stacking with other late Taxol pathway genes could enable 10-deacetylbaccatin III and paclitaxel production.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly: GCA_053640655.1; metabolomics of P. chienii, T. wallichiana, T. grandis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a core causal pathway gene in the evolutionary comparison despite being absent in P. chienii.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for oxetane formation and completion of paclitaxel branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an oxidative rearrangement involved in paclitaxel oxetane formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key late pathway enzyme; essential missing step in P. chienii
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its absence is proposed to contribute to failure of P. chienii to complete paclitaxel biosynthesis and to favor taxusin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics/pathway reconstruction; metabolomic comparison among Taxaceae species
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Absence prevents full Taxol pathway completion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TOT1/CYP1 was not detected in P. chienii. 2. Its absence, together with lack of TBT, is proposed to explain the incomplete Taxol pathway in P. chienii. 3. Blocking at TOT1/CYP1 may redirect metabolic flux toward taxusin and related analogs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important gene to introduce together with TBT for pathway completion in Pseudotaxus or heterologous production platforms.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats TOT1 and CYP1 as the same enzyme/gene nomenclature.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive correlation with taxane biosynthesis genes under MeJA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate MeJA-responsive regulator of taxane/taxusin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to promote MeJA-induced taxane biosynthesis and taxusin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated genomic/transcriptomic analysis; co-expression analysis; promoter cis-element analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μM MeJA for 24 h and 48 h
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for elicitor-responsive enhancement of taxane production in engineering systems.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted regulator only; no direct binding assay reported.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB12 is among 78 MeJA-responsive TFs identified in P. chienii. 2. Co-expression analysis links it to taxane biosynthesis-related genes. 3. It is proposed as a potential regulator of MeJA-induced taxane biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive correlation with taxane biosynthesis genes under MeJA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate MeJA-responsive regulator of taxane/taxusin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to promote MeJA-induced taxane biosynthesis and taxusin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated genomic/transcriptomic analysis; co-expression analysis; promoter cis-element analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μM MeJA for 24 h and 48 h
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for elicitor-responsive enhancement of taxane production.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted regulator only; no direct binding assay reported.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB13 is MeJA responsive in P. chienii. 2. Co-expression suggests involvement in taxane biosynthesis regulation. 3. It is proposed as a candidate regulator of taxusin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: putative transcriptional activation via G-box-associated regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: taxane biosynthesis-related genes with G-box elements in promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive correlation with taxane biosynthesis genes under MeJA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate MeJA-responsive regulator of taxane/taxusin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to enhance MeJA-induced expression of taxane biosynthesis genes and taxusin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated genomic/transcriptomic analysis; co-expression analysis; promoter cis-element analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μM MeJA for 24 h and 48 h
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate TF for MeJA-responsive taxane pathway engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted regulator only; no direct transactivation assay reported.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bHLH18 is among the MeJA-responsive TFs identified in P. chienii. 2. G-box motifs are enriched in promoters of taxane biosynthesis-related genes. 3. bHLH18 is proposed as a candidate regulator of MeJA-induced taxane biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: putative transcriptional activation via W-box-associated regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: taxane biosynthesis-related genes with W-box elements in promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive correlation with taxane biosynthesis genes under MeJA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate MeJA-responsive regulator of taxane/taxusin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to promote taxane biosynthesis gene expression under MeJA and contribute to taxusin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated genomic/transcriptomic analysis; co-expression analysis; promoter cis-element analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μM MeJA for 24 h and 48 h
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate TF for regulatory engineering of taxane production.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted regulator only; direct binding to target promoters was not shown.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY6 is a MeJA-responsive TF identified in P. chienii. 2. W-box elements are present in promoters of taxane biosynthesis-related genes. 3. WRKY6 is proposed as a potential regulator of taxane biosynthesis.
